evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Maintain documentation, lineage and data quality testing across the modelling layer.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals (dbt tests, documentation, lineage, data quality testing) balance against velocity signals ("we move fast, take bold bets", "dynamic environment", "tight deadlines"), giving parity.
evidence.domain_risk: we're on a mission to enable farmers and graziers to run the most productive and sustainable operations
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Agritech GTM analytics with no financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance language anywhere in the JD.
evidence.collaboration_width: Data Engineering; Data Scientist; Tableau analysts; BI
evidence.data_team_maturity: Partner with Data Engineering on upstream pipeline design, and with the Data Scientist and Tableau analysts on downstream consumption.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools and adjacent data roles appear in the responsibilities section, indicating a growing but not yet fully specialised data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Experience working with AWS-based analytical stacks (S3, Athena, Glue, or similar) and CI/CD-based deployment with GitHub.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise tool stack (dbt layers, AWS S3/Athena/Glue, GTM platforms) gives a concrete picture of the day-to-day work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: own the semantic layer between raw data and the business — trusted, modelled, analytics-ready datasets
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Emphasis is on serving BI/analyst/data-science consumers via a shared semantic layer, the defining internal_data pattern.
evidence.autonomy_level: contribute to the strategic direction and future of analytics at Halter
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strategic ownership language ("own the semantic layer", "contribute to strategic direction") is paired with execution-oriented partnering ("working with stakeholders to define", "partner with Data Engineering").
evidence.ai_role: power everything from AI analytics agents to reports and dashboards
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate builds the metrics/semantic layer that downstream AI agents consume, making AI the consumer of the candidate's infrastructure.
evidence.testing_framing: Maintain documentation, lineage and data quality testing across the modelling layer.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing and data quality are listed as an owned responsibility of the role, not just a tool in a skills list.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Maintain documentation, lineage and data quality testing across the modelling layer.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Reliability of the modelling layer is a stated concern but is secondary to the delivery- and product-oriented framing of the role.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: help build the foundations of our GTM data stack
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role both builds new foundational infrastructure (semantic layer, GTM data stack) and maintains/extends existing dbt models.
evidence.work_arrangement: We're office first, not office only. Working from the office is our default, but we flex when needed.
evidence.loss_aversion: Maintain documentation, lineage and data quality testing across the modelling layer. — reflects concern for pipeline/data reliability rather than compliance or regulatory risk.
